item.title: Shengwen Gan, Danqing He, Xiaochun Li, Shukun Wu - On Local Smoothing For Wave Equations
item.description: Introduction This paper obtains sharp estimates for local smoothing estimates for the wave equation, and related Fourier integral operators satisfying a positive definite cinematic curvature condition.

item.title: Local Smoothing Inequality For The Wave Equation
item.description: In 1980, Akihiko Miyachi and Juan Carlos Peral separately proved that the half-wave propagator e^{it \sqrt{-\Delta}} on \mathbb{R}^d is bounded from L^p_s(\mathbb{R}^d) to L^\infty_{t,\text{loc}} L^p_x for s \geq (d-1)|1/p - 1/2|.

item.title: 0. Questions
item.description: The linkage between fine scales and coarse scales is provided (heuristically, at least) by the dispersion relation, which asserts that the frequency of a wave at fine scales determines the (group) velocity of that wave at coarse scales; in the physical interpretation of the Schr¨odinger equation, th...

item.title: Kolmogorov-Seliverstov-Plessner Linearization
item.description: The act of studying a maximal operator \sup_\theta A_\theta on a space X by considering arbitrary functions \theta: X \to \Theta, and considering the linear operator A_\theta f(x) = A_{\theta(x)} f(x).

item.title: Relations Between Local Smoothing and Restriction
item.description: Rogers showed that local smoothing estimates for the Schrödinger equation are equivalent to restriction estimates for the parabola.

item.title: Circular Averages
item.description: For d \geq 2. let A_r denote the convolution operator with the surface measure of the circle of radius r at the origin.

item.title: Rapidly Decaying Wave Packet Weights
item.description: TODO Using translation invariance, can go from local estimates to weighted estimates, so that the inequalities can be composed.

item.title: Removing Exceptional Sets From Lp Estimates
item.description: Suppose X_0 has finite measure, and we have to prove an inequality of the form \| f \|_{L^p(X_0)} \lesssim C.

item.title: We Can Remove Exceptional Sets From Lp Estimates
item.description: Suppose X_0 has finite measure, and we have to prove an inequality of the form \| f \|_{L^p(X_0)} \lesssim C.

item.title: Important Exponents in Restriction Theory
item.description: Here we are working with restriction operators for curved hypersurfaces of dimension n in \mathbb{R}^d.
